title: hieracium pilosella subsp. flagellare
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. flagellare, also known as the common hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow flowers and spreading habit. The plant is rich in flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Hieracium pilosella subsp. flagellare may help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant protection, contributing to overall health and well-being. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
